pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos from the james webb telescope and it just hit me... like, everything we do, every single thing, is just a tiny, accidental blip in an endless, cold void. it all feels so incredibly pointless. if the universe is just going to end in heat death anyway, what is even the point of trying to exist? it feels like we're just performing a play for an empty theater.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im typing this. everything just feels... heavy. like i've spent 5 years building this version of myself around my research and now that the funding is gone and the lab results are a joke, there's nothing left. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting to keep my head above water. i just want everything to stop. i just want some kind of peace that isnt just sleeping for a few hours.

pool.baseline: The candles flicker low, and the rain against the window pane sounds like the weeping of a thousand ghosts. It is all over, is it not? The betrayal... it has left my heart an empty, hollow thing. I look at the dawn approaching and I feel nothing but a profound, aching emptiness. There is no music left in this world for me, only this suffocating silence. I feel as though I am already fading, becoming a shadow among shadows, and part of me... part of me longs to simply let the darkness take me completely.
